what_is_changing: Add ProjectKnowledgeIndex and related knowledge-record primitives as PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority records.
Reconcile WP-KERNEL-009 touched Loom, search, workflow, Shadow Workspace, and AI-ready text with the active no-SQLite Kernel V1 law.
Lock Tiptap/ProseMirror plus Monaco as bundled Handshake editor libraries integrated into product authority, not as external apps.
Define Loom/Obsidian views as projections over canonical knowledge records, not markdown vault authority.
Define UserManual as the model/operator manual surface and classify legacy ModelManual paths as compatibility or migration targets.
Add backend navigation, visual-debug, negative fixture, and HBR acceptance anchors for no-context and parallel-agent operation.
why: The active v02.191 spec contains both newer PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority law and older SQLite-first Loom/search/workflow text in WP-KERNEL-009 touched areas.
WP-KERNEL-009 folds six legacy stubs and should not launch implementation with conflicting storage, retrieval, and manual-surface authority.
No-context local/cloud models need backend navigation, UserManual, receipts, and visual evidence as product requirements before the packet can be hydrated honestly.
The rich editor and knowledge index are foundational surfaces; fixing authority now reduces rework across later retrieval, thinking-pipeline, and trace-bundle packets.
architectural_impacts: PostgreSQL/EventLedger becomes the only durable authority path for WP-KERNEL-009 knowledge records, rich docs, graph edges, claims, retrieval traces, UserManual entries, and validation receipts.
Generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, and debug exports become projection-only artifacts with source links back to typed authority records.
CRDT state remains the collaborative editing transport, but authoritative promotion, replay, and validation land through EventLedger/PostgreSQL.
Tiptap/ProseMirror document JSON and Monaco code-node payloads must have versioned schemas and round-trip tests.
Loom view filters, backlinks, graph traversal, and retrieval modes consume canonical records and must expose typed BackendNavigation receipts for parallel models.
UserManual moves from naming convention to product contract; manual self-consistency becomes a build/handoff requirement.

changes: Add ProjectKnowledgeIndex primitive family and canonical records: KnowledgeSource, KnowledgeSpan, KnowledgeEntity, KnowledgeEdge, KnowledgeClaim, MemoryPassage, RetrievalTrace, RichDocument, EditorCodeNode, UserManualRecord.
Reconcile Shadow Workspace, knowledge graph, AI-ready data, workflow durability, and Loom sections with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority and no-SQLite fallback.
Classify old SQLite references as legacy product debt where not directly removed.
Lock Tiptap/ProseMirror plus Monaco as the integrated Handshake rich editor stack for WP-KERNEL-009.
Define required controls for rich text, code blocks, media embeds, file/folder/project links, backlinks, tags, and AI edit proposals.
Require responsive visual-debug verification for editor, graph, manual, and error states.
stale_conflict_examples: Current rich editor guidance exists but does not yet bind WP-KERNEL-009 to UserManual, backend navigation, and Postgres/EventLedger authority.

changes: Add Project Knowledge Index product surface.
Add Rich Editor and Monaco code-node product surface.
Add UserManual product surface and migration bridge from ModelManual.
Add backend navigation routes and visual-debug requirements for no-context models.
Preserve Loom detail while changing authority language away from SQLite compatibility/fallback for WP-KERNEL-009.
State that Loom browsing views are projections over canonical ProjectKnowledgeIndex and Loom records.
Update search, graph traversal, realtime, and schema examples to avoid SQLite-as-proof wording.
Add primitive registry entries for the WP-KERNEL-009 primitive family.
Add interaction-matrix edges between ProjectKnowledgeIndex, Loom, RichDocument, EditorCodeNode, CRDT, EventLedger, ContextBundle, UserManual, BackendNavigation, VisualDebugEvidence, and HBR rows.
Add negative fixture and validator evidence anchors.
stale_conflict_examples: Appendices must resolve new primitive IDs introduced by the enriched main-body sections.

not_in_scope: No product code implementation in this enrichment confirmation step.
No broad cleanup of every old SQLite mention in the entire Master Spec unless it intersects WP-KERNEL-009 authority.
No new work packet variant unless operator changes scope.
No ACP launch, no coder launch, no validator launch, and no WP worktree creation before enrichment refresh.
No default Markdown projection or sidecar authority.

risks_and_controls.risk: Spec patch becomes an unrelated broad rewrite.
risks_and_controls.control: Limit v02.192 edits to the five modules and listed WP-KERNEL-009 authority conflicts.
risks_and_controls.verification: Review module diff and changelog changed_modules list.

risks_and_controls.risk: Signature token is made unusable by premature persistence.
risks_and_controls.control: Do not write the exact token to repo files until the final post-enrichment signature gate.
risks_and_controls.verification: Before final signature, grep for the exact token must still return no repo matches.

risks_and_controls.risk: Imported Loom detail loses useful behavior while removing SQLite fallback text.
risks_and_controls.control: Preserve Loom block, edge, backlink, view, and retrieval semantics; change authority/storage language only where needed.
risks_and_controls.verification: Spec diff confirms Loom behavior rows remain while authority wording changes.

risks_and_controls.risk: UserManual migration breaks old product routes.
risks_and_controls.control: Require explicit compatibility shims or migration rows for existing model_manual paths.
risks_and_controls.verification: Manual self-consistency and no-context harness tests cover renamed surfaces.

required_validation_after_approval: json parse of refinement.json and spec-enrichment-proposal.json

approval_request.prompt: Confirm this Spec Proposal before the Activation Manager creates master-spec-v02.192 and updates SPEC_CURRENT.
approval_request.signature_note: The final one-time signature is intentionally not consumed until the refreshed refinement declares ENRICHMENT_NEEDED=NO.

operator_approval_evidence: Operator chat approval on 2026-06-11: APPROVE SPEC PROPOSAL WP-KERNEL-009 v02.192

validation_results.notes: spec-debt-registry, spec-eof-appendices, spec-growth-discipline, and spec-governance-reference checks passed.
Required validator anchors resolve against v02.192.
Appendix machine-readable spec_version/path projections resolve against v02.192.
Repo-wide generated projection drift remains in WP-KERNEL-005 MT/packet/refinement projections; no WP-KERNEL-009 packet or MT projections exist yet.
